description: A vertical structure forming side of building or enclosure; the standing boundary that defines space. 'Moru' names what stands between—the solid vertical that creates inside.
sound_symbolism: The word stands solid. 'Mo' grounds with stable mass, wall planted. Then 'ru' runs continuous, barrier extending.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'moru' (the wall). Creates space when combined with 'toru' (roof), 'tomae' (floor). The vertical boundary.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Boundaries as skillful means—walls protect without imprisoning, define without dividing hearts.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Walls created shelter—the first architecture separating protected from exposed.
